name: MCT Oil
summary: MCT Oil is best framed as a conservative research profile with mechanistic evidence and a focus on Mitochondrial Health; Metabolic Health; Energy Metabolism. Current workbook signals support mechanistic context around Metabolic Regulation; Lipid Metabolism Support, not treatment claims.
description: MCT Oil is best framed as a conservative research profile with mechanistic evidence and a focus on Mitochondrial Health; Metabolic Health; Energy Metabolism. Current workbook signals support mechanistic context around Metabolic Regulation; Lipid Metabolism Support, not treatment claims.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v10.3: MCT effects on body composition are modest/context-dependent; emphasize tolerance and calorie context.
claimMap.notes: v10.3: MCT effects on body composition are modest/context-dependent; emphasize tolerance and calorie context.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: MCT oil dosing is product/tolerance-specific; start low (for example 5–15 mL) to reduce GI effects

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: PubMed PMID 18326600. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 18326600. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: Cirrhosis or hepatic encephalopathy or portal-systemic shunting (MCT metabolism can worsen ketone and ammonia handling)
poorly controlled or type 1 diabetes (ketogenic effect may exacerbate ketoacidosis risk)
rapid introduction or high doses (dose-dependent GI upset such as cramping or diarrhea or nausea)
